kaResponse.analysis: OpenShift Route 'storefront' points to a non-existent backend service 'storefront-web-v2', causing RouteBackendUnavailable. The Route was patched via kubectl at 2026-05-06T21:20:49Z (generation 2), changing spec.to.name from 'storefront-web' to 'storefront-web-v2'. The service 'storefront-web-v2' was never created in the 'demo-route' namespace. The correct backend service 'storefront-web' exists and its 2 pods are fully healthy. Fix: patch the Route's spec.to.name back to 'storefront-web'.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: OpenShift Route 'storefront' points to a non-existent backend service 'storefront-web-v2', causing RouteBackendUnavailable. The Route was patched via kubectl at 2026-05-06T21:20:49Z (generation 2), changing spec.to.name from 'storefront-web' to 'storefront-web-v2'. The service 'storefront-web-v2' was never created in the 'demo-route' namespace. The correct backend service 'storefront-web' exists and its 2 pods are fully healthy. Fix: patch the Route's spec.to.name back to 'storefront-web'.
causalChain: Signal: RouteBackendUnavailable on Route 'storefront' in namespace 'demo-route'
Why? The OpenShift router cannot find any endpoints for the Route's backend service 'storefront-web-v2'
Why? Service 'storefront-web-v2' does not exist in the 'demo-route' namespace — only 'storefront-web' exists

Why? The Route was patched (kubectl-patch, generation 2, timestamp 2026-05-06T21:20:49Z) changing spec.to.name from 'storefront-web' to 'storefront-web-v2' without a corresponding service being created

Root cause: Route 'storefront' spec.to.name was incorrectly set to 'storefront-web-v2' via kubectl patch, pointing to a non-existent service while the correct service 'storefront-web' and its healthy pods remain available

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: The Route 'storefront' is the correct remediation target — its spec.to.name field needs to be changed from 'storefront-web-v2' back to 'storefront-web'. The backend pods and existing service are healthy and require no changes.
dueDiligence.proportionality: The remediation is a single field change (spec.to.name) on the Route — highly targeted and proportional. No other resources require modification.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: Route, all Services in namespace, all Pods in namespace, Deployment, Route events, Namespace events, resource context/remediation history. All resources mentioned in or implied by the signal were examined.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Fully traced: RouteBackendUnavailable → missing backend service → Route points to 'storefront-web-v2' → service never created → Route was patched to wrong service name. Cannot go deeper without access to change management records explaining why the patch was applied.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by direct tool output: Route spec confirmed via kubectl_find_resource and kubectl_describe showing spec.to.name='storefront-web-v2'; Service list confirmed only 'storefront-web' exists; Deployment and Pod status confirmed 2/2 healthy replicas; Route managedFields confirm kubectl-patch operation at generation 2.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history exists for Route 'storefront' in either tier1 (24h) or tier2 (90d) windows. N/A — no regression risk from prior failed remediations.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Pods unhealthy — ruled out, both pods are Running with all conditions True and 0 restarts. (2) Service selector mismatch — ruled out, 'storefront-web' service correctly selects app=storefront-web pods. (3) Network/CNI issue — ruled out, pods have valid OVN-Kubernetes network assignments. (4) TLS misconfiguration — ruled out, Route status shows 'Admitted' by the router.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: Starting at 1.0: -0.03 for no direct Prometheus metrics confirming the exact duration/scope of traffic impact; -0.02 for not having access to change management records explaining the intent of the kubectl-patch operation. Final confidence: 0.95.

contributingFactors: Route was patched (kubectl-patch at 2026-05-06T21:20:49Z, generation 2) changing spec.to.name from 'storefront-web' to 'storefront-web-v2'
Service 'storefront-web-v2' was never created in the demo-route namespace
The actual backend Deployment 'storefront-web' and its 2 pods are fully healthy with 0 restarts
The existing service 'storefront-web' correctly selects pods with label app=storefront-web on port 8080
